title_ar: مقدمة ابن الصلاح — صيغ الأداء والتحمل ورواية الحديث
content_en:
One of the most enduring contributions of Ibn al-Salah's Muqaddimah is the standardization and explanation of the technical vocabulary of hadith science — the specialized terminology that allows scholars to communicate with precision about the status, characteristics, and usage of individual narrations. This chapter surveys the most important terms in that vocabulary, providing definitions that became the reference standard for all subsequent scholarship.

Among the most important terms related to the chain of narration (isnad): 'Musnad' — a narration with a complete chain reaching back to the Prophet ﷺ; 'Marfu'' — a narration attributed specifically to the Prophet ﷺ (whether in chain or content); 'Mawquf' — a narration stopping at a Companion without being attributed to the Prophet ﷺ; 'Maqtu'' — a narration stopping at a Successor (Tabi'i); 'Mu'an'an' — a narration in which the narrator uses the formula 'an' (from) rather than stating 'I heard', raising questions about the continuity of the chain; 'Mudallas' — a narration in which the narrator conceals something about the chain.

Terms related to the number of narrators at each level: 'Mutawatir' (see chapter 5); 'Mashhur' (famous/widely known) — transmitted by three or more narrators at each level but not reaching tawatur; 'Aziz' (scarce) — transmitted by exactly two narrators at each level; 'Gharib' (strange/singular) — having only one narrator at some level.

Terms related to narrator evaluation: 'Thiqah' (trustworthy) — the highest standard of reliability; 'Saduq' (truthful) — reliable but of a slightly lower standard; 'La ba'sa bihi' (no objection to him) — acceptable, with minor reservations; 'Layyin' (soft) — slightly weak; 'Da'if' (weak) — significantly unreliable; 'Matruk' (abandoned) — narrations from this person are generally rejected; 'Matruh' (rejected) — narrations are rejected entirely; 'Kadhdhaab' (liar) and 'Wada'' (fabricator) — the most severe criticism, applied to those who invented hadiths.

Terms related to transmission modes: 'Sama'' (hearing) — the narrator heard directly from the teacher; 'Ijazah' (permission) — the teacher gave written or verbal permission to transmit their collection without the student having heard all of it; 'Munawalah' (handing over) — the teacher physically gave their written materials to the student; 'Wijadah' (finding) — the narrator found hadiths in writing without having received them directly; 'Wasiyyah' (bequest) — the teacher bequeathed their books to the student at death.

Terms related to textual analysis: 'Matn' — the text/content of the hadith; 'Illah' — a hidden defect in the chain or text; 'Shadhdhudhh' — anomaly (a reliable narrator contradicting more reliable narrators); 'Mudtarib' (confused) — contradictory narrations of equal authority; 'Maqlub' (overturned) — names or phrases in the hadith are transposed; 'Mudraj' (interpolated) — words added to the text of the hadith from another source, usually explained.

This technical vocabulary represents not merely academic categorization but a precise ethical framework for the preservation of prophetic speech — each term carving out a distinct space in the epistemology of religious knowledge.
content_id:
Salah satu kontribusi paling abadi dari Muqaddimah Ibn ash-Shalah adalah standarisasi dan penjelasan kosakata teknis ilmu hadis — terminologi khusus yang memungkinkan para ulama berkomunikasi dengan presisi tentang status, karakteristik, dan penggunaan narasi-narasi individual dalam tradisi hadis.

Di antara istilah-istilah penting yang distandarisasi oleh Ibn ash-Shalah:

Isnad / Sanad: Rantai periwayatan yang menghubungkan penulis kitab hadis hingga kepada Nabi ﷺ melalui serangkaian perawi.

Matan: Teks atau konten utama dari hadis — apa yang sebenarnya dikatakan atau dilakukan oleh Nabi ﷺ.

Rawi: Perawi — orang yang meriwayatkan hadis.

Mursal: Hadis yang Tabi'innya langsung meriwayatkan dari Nabi ﷺ tanpa menyebutkan Sahabat di antaranya.

Munqathi': Hadis yang rantai periwayatannya terputus di bagian tengah.

Mu'allaq: Hadis yang rantai periwayatannya terputus di awal — penulis kitab menghilangkan satu atau lebih perawi dari awal sanadnya.

Mudallas: Hadis di mana perawi menyamarkan kenyataan bahwa ia tidak benar-benar mendengar dari gurunya secara langsung.

Masyhur: Hadis yang pada mulanya diriwayatkan melalui satu atau dua jalur tetapi kemudian menjadi sangat terkenal dan tersebar luas.

Gharib: Hadis yang hanya diriwayatkan melalui satu jalur periwayatan.

Mawdhu': Hadis palsu — diciptakan dan dinisbahkan kepada Nabi ﷺ secara tidak benar.

Kosakata teknis ini memungkinkan para ulama untuk mendiskusikan hadis dengan presisi tinggi dan memastikan bahwa evaluasi mereka dapat dipahami dengan tepat oleh ulama lainnya.
content_ar:
في كتاب "مقدمة ابن الصلاح" في علوم الحديث، يتناول المؤلف في هذا الجزء نوعًا من أنواع علوم الحديث النبوي الشريف مما له صلة بالموضوع المعنون أعلاه، مستعينًا بالأدلة الشرعية من الكتاب والسنة وأقوال الأئمة والعلماء.

ويُفصّل المؤلف في هذا القسم المسائل الواردة تفصيلًا وافيًا، مع الإشارة إلى وجوه الخلاف بين العلماء ومواضع الاتفاق، مُستندًا إلى منهج أهل العلم في التحقيق والتدقيق والاستدلال بالدليل الصحيح.

ويُركّز المؤلف على استيعاب الفروع الفقهية والمسائل العلمية المتعلقة بهذا الموضوع، موضّحًا الحِكَم والعلل الشرعية حيث أمكن، مما يُعين القارئ على الفهم الصحيح والتطبيق السليم لما يتعلمه من أحكام ومسائل.

ويختتم هذا الجزء بخلاصة جامعة للمسائل التي تناولها المؤلف، مع الإشارة إلى المراجع والمصادر التي استقى منها معلوماته، وهو ما يُعزز الثقة بمضمون الكتاب ويجعله مرجعًا موثوقًا في بابه.
